name: Adelle
classification_notes: Multipurpose slab serif with sturdy serifs and slightly dark colour; designed for intensive editorial use across print and digital
summary: A workhorse slab serif designed for editorial use, with one of the broadest script extension programmes in the independent type world.
description: Adelle pairs sturdy slab serifs with a slightly dark texture that holds up under demanding print conditions. Its eight-weight Latin range is complemented by Condensed and Mono companions, plus extensions across fourteen scripts covering Arabic, Chinese, Devanagari and beyond. Winner of the Gold European Design Award in 2010.
foundry_blurb: A multipurpose slab serif with unobtrusive appearance, excellent texture, and slightly dark colour — designed for intensive editorial use.

name: Adelle Sans
classification_notes: Humanist grotesque companion to Adelle; clean but spirited with strong branding and signage credentials
summary: A clean, spirited grotesque designed as a sans companion to Adelle, with nine-script coverage making it one of the most multilingual sans-serifs available.
description: Adelle Sans maintains the proportional harmony of its slab-serif sibling while offering the neutrality needed for branding, signage and digital platforms. Thirty styles span eight weights with matching italics in both regular and condensed widths. Named one of Typographica's Best Typefaces of 2012.
foundry_blurb: A clean and spirited take on the traditional grotesque sans, designed for flexibility across branding, signage, advertising, and digital platforms.

name: Adelle Mono
classification_notes: Monospaced family with a Flex variant offering proportional widths; variable font with weight and MONO axes
designer: José Scaglione, Veronika Burian & Irene Vlachou
summary: A monospaced companion to Adelle with a Flex variant that modulates between fixed and proportional widths via a variable font axis.
description: Adelle Mono offers twenty styles across five weights with italics, split between true monospaced and proportional Flex variants. The variable font's MONO axis allows designers and developers to shift between grid-locked and free-spaced widths in a single file. Suited to code editors, branding and editorial contexts alike.
foundry_blurb: Twenty styles designed with both the developer and the aesthete in mind, offering monospaced width for coding and proportional width for design applications.

name: Bree
classification_notes: Spirited upright italic sans with calligraphic influences; distinctive single-story 'a' and cursive 'e'; designed for branding and headlines
summary: A playful upright italic sans with handwriting roots, seven weights and script extensions spanning six writing systems.
description: Bree's single-story 'a', cursive 'e' and wide stance give it personality without sacrificing versatility. The Latin family spans seven weights with matching obliques, and the type system extends to Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, Thai and Devanagari. Variable font support was added in recent updates. Won the 2009 European Design Festival Bronze Award.
foundry_blurb: A spirited upright italic sans influenced by handwriting, featuring a pleasant mix of rather unobtrusive capitals with more vivid lowercase letters.

name: Bree Serif
classification_notes: Slab serif companion to Bree; preserves the parent's handwriting character while adding editorial weight through slab letterforms
summary: The slab-serif companion to Bree, retaining the family's handwriting energy across twelve weights with italics.
description: Bree Serif carries forward the playful character of the sans parent, trading open terminals for sturdy slabs that suit editorial and branding work at heavier weights. Twelve styles cover Thin through ExtraBold with matching italics, plus a full complement of OpenType features. Won Gold at the European Design Awards in 2014.
foundry_blurb: An energetic and mature slab serif that preserves handwriting characteristics while supporting editorial applications.

name: Karmina
classification_notes: Sturdy newspaper serif with inktraps, large x-height, and compressed proportions; engineered for low-quality paper and high-speed printing
summary: A calligraphically influenced newspaper serif with generous inktraps and compressed proportions built to perform on challenging print substrates.
description: Karmina was engineered from the outset for low-quality papers, high-speed presses and inconsistent ink coverage. Inktraps at acute connections, a large x-height and compressed letterforms combine to maximise readability per column inch. Four styles pair with the Karmina Sans companion. Won Merit at the 2007 European Design Awards.
foundry_blurb: Engineered for challenging print environments — reliable on low-quality papers, high-speed web presses, and with inconsistent ink levels.

name: Karmina Sans
classification_notes: Versatile text sans-serif companion to Karmina; medium weights for body text, extreme weights for display; first prize Granshan 2009
summary: The sans-serif companion to Karmina, ranging from delicate text weights to one of the darkest, most powerful heavyweights in the TypeTogether catalogue.
description: Karmina Sans covers twelve styles from Light to Heavy with italics, bridging body-text legibility and headline impact within a single family. The extreme weights — particularly Heavy — deliver exceptional colour and punch for corporate identity work. Variable font support is available. First prize at the 2009 Granshan Type Design Competition.
foundry_blurb: A versatile and vivid text face — medium weights for easy reading, extreme weights for headlines and corporate identity.

name: Soleil
classification_notes: Geometric sans-serif with warm humanist corrections; originated as a wayfinding commission; large x-height and generous counters
summary: A geometric sans-serif with a humanist warmth developed for a Viennese signage commission, now available as a variable font with updated Vietnamese and Pinyin support.
description: Soleil balances mathematical precision with fluid rhythm, using generous counters, tall ascenders and a large x-height suited to wayfinding and editorial work alike. Seven weights with matching italics plus a Magic Caps display variant make up the family. Won Silver at the 2012 Austrian Joseph Binder Award.
foundry_blurb: A geometric sans that plays by the rules, but only up to a certain point — originally designed for a 1950s modernist building signage system in Vienna.

name: Capitolium 2
classification_notes: Three-series newsface with Text, regular and Headline optical sizes; classic letterforms with newsprint-specific adjustments; Gerard Unger design
summary: Gerard Unger's three-series newsface optimised for newsprint — with dedicated Text, standard and Headline subfamilies tuned to each optical size.
description: Capitolium 2 provides a complete editorial system in 19 styles across three subfamilies, each calibrated for a specific size range: News for body text, standard for general editorial, Headline for display. Features include 350-plus glyphs per weight, four numeral sets, small caps and arbitrary fractions. Won the Letter.2 competition and featured in Typographica's Best Typefaces of 2011.
foundry_blurb: Classic letterforms tweaked for the stringent requirements of newsprint — sturdier shapes and more characters per line of text.

name: Fino
classification_notes: Dramatic contemporary Didone with narrow proportions, sharp details and high contrast; includes Sans and Stencil subfamilies
summary: A theatrical contemporary Didone with narrow proportions and high contrast, offering Sans and Stencil companions for versatile titling applications.
description: Fino amplifies the dramatic qualities of the Didone genre through sharp hairlines, extreme weight contrast and narrow letterforms that command attention at display sizes. Title versions with finer hairlines are tuned for larger applications. Twenty-four fonts across three subfamilies — Serif, Sans and Stencil — give designers considerable range.
foundry_blurb: A dramatic, contemporary Didone titling font family that scales beyond the world of looks by tapping into archetypes.

name: Marco
classification_notes: Lively humanist serif with strong calligraphic disposition and 15th-century Italian influences; Cyrillic and Greek extensions available
summary: A lively humanist serif by Toshi Omagari with calligraphic roots in 15th-century Italian letterforms, extended to Cyrillic and polytonic Greek.
description: Marco draws on the traditions of Nicolas Jenson and Aldus Manutius, rendering them in six styles with a contemporary freshness and a touch of informality. Each style carries over 1,900 characters with extensive OpenType features including swashes, small caps and multiple figure sets. Cyrillic and polytonic Greek companions extend its editorial range.
foundry_blurb: A lively, humanist serif family with a touch of informality — strong calligraphic disposition, angled asymmetric baseline serifs, and slightly flared stems.

name: Athelas
classification_notes: Elegant book serif with open counters and graceful serifs; used in Apple iBooks and macOS; Cyrillic and Greek extensions available
summary: An elegant four-style book serif chosen by Apple for iBooks and macOS, with Cyrillic, Greek and Arabic companion extensions.
description: Athelas prioritises reading comfort through open counters, fluid letterforms and graceful serifs suited equally to screen and print. Its four-style Latin core — Regular, Italic, Bold and Bold Italic — is extended by separate Cyrillic, monotonic Greek and Arabic companions. First prize at the 2008 Granshan Type Design Competition.
foundry_blurb: A book font family that breathes peacefully on the page to usher the reader into the wordsmith's art.

name: Portada
classification_notes: Screen-optimised serif with sturdy serifs, wide proportions and generous x-height; Arabic and Thai extensions available
summary: A serif engineered for long-form reading on screens and in apps, with separate Text optimised styles and multilingual Arabic and Thai extensions.
description: Portada combines sturdy serifs with generous x-height and wide proportions to achieve screen clarity comparable to many sans-serifs. The 21-font family splits into display and Text subsets, and companion Arabic and Thai families extend its reach for multilingual digital publishing. Won the ED-Awards Silver and Clap Award Platinum in 2016-17.
foundry_blurb: Designed for superlative performance in long-form reading on screens, in UI, and in apps.

name: Ebony
classification_notes: Multi-functional humanist grotesque balancing grotesque regularity with humanist openness; 18 styles from Thin to UltraBlack
summary: A multi-functional grotesque spanning nine weights from Thin to UltraBlack, with open terminals and powerful curves suited to branding and advertising.
description: Ebony balances humanist openness with grotesque regularity across 18 styles with distinctive wide stances and open terminals that sharpen into crisp angles at heavier weights. The ultra-dark weights deliver strong presence for advertising and identity work. Vietnamese tonal support and variable font formats are included. Selected for Tipos Latinos 2016.
foundry_blurb: A multi-functional type family that remains true to TypeTogether's focus on stylish typographic answers for the complex needs of printed and digital works.

name: Protipo
classification_notes: Low-contrast sans designed for information design; four widths (Compact, Narrow, Regular, Wide) plus icon sets; variable font with wght and wdth axes
summary: A systematic information-design sans spanning four widths and eight weights with icon sets and a variable font covering both weight and width axes.
description: Protipo is built for the demands of wayfinding, transit, apps and infographics, offering four widths from Compact to Wide with up to eight weights each. Fifty-two styles in total include two icon weights, making it a self-contained information design system. Variable font technology allows continuous width and weight adjustment from a single file.
foundry_blurb: A low-contrast sans-serif family designed for information designers — excelling in apps, infographics, UI, wayfinding, transport, posters and display.

name: Crete
classification_notes: Unconventional slab serif varying serif weight rather than stem width across styles; inspired by Greek chapel lettering; free on Google Fonts
summary: A distinctive display slab that varies serif weight rather than stem width to create three stylistically distinct but metrically interchangeable styles.
description: Crete's unconventional approach — three serif variants (Thin, Round, Thick) that share identical metrics while differing in serif treatment — makes them freely interchangeable in layout without reflowing text. Inspired by wall lettering in a Greek chapel, it won First Prize at the 2008 Granshan Type Design Competition. Available under SIL Open Source Licence via Google Fonts.
foundry_blurb: A distinctive slab serif with a sense of movement throughout each glyph, distinguished by varying serif weight rather than stem width across its three styles.

name: Atlante
classification_notes: Blends flat pen and flexible pen influences; Display and Text optical subfamilies with 36 styles total; TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence
summary: An expressive serif blending flat-pen and flexible-pen calligraphic traditions, with separate Display and Text optical families across nine weights.
description: Atlante combines traditional calligraphic energy with a contemporary sensibility in 36 styles split between Display and Text subfamilies, each optimised for its intended size range. Eleven stylistic sets, extensive swashes and alternates give designers fine control over personality and formality. Won the 2022 Clap Platinum Award and the TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence.
foundry_blurb: An emotional serif typeface blending flat pen and flexible pen influences, offering conventional but excessive styling with extensive stylistic variations.

name: LFT Etica Sheriff
classification_notes: Versatile slab serif with regular, condensed and compressed widths; 36 styles total; commissions origin by Leftloft Milan
summary: A personality-driven slab serif in three widths — Regular, Condensed and Compressed — equally effective in headlines and body copy.
description: LFT Etica Sheriff delivers 36 styles across its three width variants, each with six weights and matching italics. Developed from a commissioned project by Leftloft Milan and refined with Octavio Pardo, it attracts attention at headline scale while remaining legible and plainspoken in paragraph-length text.
foundry_blurb: A slab serif that equally attracts the reader with its headline swagger and retains its plainspokenness in conversational paragraphs.

name: Ploquine
classification_notes: Slab serif with unusual horizontal terminals on 'a' and 't'; extra-large capitals; inspired by historical wood type; 2022 Gerard Unger Scholarship winner
summary: A structured slab serif with unusual horizontal terminals and extra-large capitals, rooted in historical wood type and shaped by the Gerard Unger Scholarship.
description: Ploquine draws on wood type traditions to produce a slab with structured delicacy — the distinctive horizontal endings on 'a' and 't', oversized capitals and serifs treated as expressive elements rather than mechanical fixtures. Fourteen styles span Thin to Extrabold with italics, plus two variable fonts. Suited to editorial, museum catalogues and poster work.
foundry_blurb: A slab serif of structured delicacy inspired by historical wood type designs, balancing calm and structured aesthetics suitable for editorial and branding.

name: Dargon
classification_notes: Epic serif with uncial display and text subfamilies; six named uncial display variants; designed for gaming and fantasy applications; 2024 Gerard Unger Scholarship winner
summary: An epic serif merging uncial display variants with a modern text family — designed for fantasy gaming, RPG materials and literary publishing.
description: Dargon combines six named uncial display styles (Ignis, Limen, Mutatio, Oculus, Aegis and Umbra) with a five-weight text subfamily for body copy. The result covers everything from gaming UI headers to novel-length reading. Supports 200-plus Latin languages. Won the 2024 Gerard Unger Scholarship.
foundry_blurb: An epic typeface inspired by fantasy gaming that merges uncial roots steeped in myth with modern legibility standards.

name: Archibrazo
classification_notes: Sculptural wedge-serif with a Rotunda display variant; pays homage to Buenos Aires publisher El Archibrazo; designed by veteran Argentine type designer Rubén Fontana
summary: A sculptural wedge-serif by Rubén Fontana with a Rotunda display variant, rooted in Argentine cultural heritage and designed for effortless readability.
description: Archibrazo combines calligraphic roots with sculptural wedge serifs across seven weights, drawing inspiration from the Buenos Aires surrealist publishing house El Archibrazo. The Rotunda variant adds a round-lettered display face for titling and poster work. Thirteen fonts total, with variable font support and a full OpenType feature set.
foundry_blurb: Calligraphy combines with sculpture in master type designer Rubén Fontana's Archibrazo, a serif type family designed with effortless readability in mind.

name: Rezak
classification_notes: Incised serif with hand-cut letterform origins (paper, stone, wood); includes a unique Black Incised display style; Cyrillic, Armenian and Georgian extensions
summary: An incised serif by Anya Danilova with hand-cut origins and a distinctive Black Incised display style, extended to Cyrillic, Armenian and Georgian scripts.
description: Rezak draws on letterforms hand-cut from paper, stone and wood to produce varying contrast and angles across ten styles. The Black Incised weight takes the hand-cut aesthetic to its most expressive extreme. Script extensions to Cyrillic, Armenian and Georgian were developed with local type designers Gor Jihanian and Ana Sanikidze.
foundry_blurb: A tribute to substance and dynamism, with display, text, and incised styles undergirding each other — its distinct tone and rhythmic aesthetic is naïve or emotional, but always invigorating.

name: Adelle Sans Chinese
classification_notes: Bilingual family pairing Adelle Sans with FounderType Lanting Hei; Simplified and Traditional Chinese support across seven weights
designer: José Scaglione, Veronika Burian & FounderType Beijing
summary: A seven-weight bilingual family pairing Adelle Sans with FounderType's Lanting Hei for unified Latin and Chinese typography.
description: Adelle Sans Chinese harmonises the Latin grotesque Adelle Sans with FounderType Lanting Hei across seven weights, treating both scripts as equal partners within multiscript layouts. Simplified and Traditional Chinese are both covered. The collaboration between TypeTogether and FounderType Beijing makes cross-script editorial and branding work straightforward.
foundry_blurb: Harmonises the FounderType Lanting Hei family with seven corresponding Adelle Sans styles — a clean grotesque and a modern Hei style working as lively and versatile equals.
